product.name: ArWoWear 260 Moda Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Modacrylic Workwear Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
product.specLine: 260 g/m², 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
descriptions: Antistatic 3/1S Twill Fabric 60% modacrylic 38% cotton 2% carbon, inherent flame retardant antistatic twill fabric . ArWoWear 260 Moda Pro fabric are mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters.
It is resistant to very high sun level, with fastness level of 4 to 5.
ArWoWear 260 Moda Pro are inherent flame retardant with EN 11612 certification.
Suitable for antistatic application, certified for EN ISO 1149.
Arc flash protection with EN 61482 class 1 (4 kA) certification.
60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
260 g/m², 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
technicalParameters: Yarn | Warp:30/2 Ne; Weft:30/2 Ne | EN ISO 2060
Tensile Strength | Warp > 900N ; Weft > 700N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp > 15N ; Weft > 15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612
applicationImageLabels: Modacrylic Coverall for Oil and Gas Industry

evidence: Mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit statement that the fabric is used by welders, combined with EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification, directly supports welding application.
Explicit EN ISO 20471 high-visibility certification directly supports hi-vis application.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit mention of electrical workers as target users along with arc-flash (EN 61482) protection directly supports energy sector application.
Explicit mention of firefighters as intended users plus EN ISO 11612 heat/flame protection supports firefighting use.
No explicit military or uniformed service use mentioned in the product data.
Application image label mentions 'Oil and Gas Industry' coverall but no explicit vehicle/vessel transport use stated.

evidence: Mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters
primary.rationale: The product description explicitly states it is used by welders and carries EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification, making welding the most direct and dominant stated end use.

secondary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 20471 certification and hi-vis property independently support high-visibility use as a secondary application, distinct from the welding use.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Firefighters are mentioned among users and EN ISO 11612 is present, but welding is more explicitly the leading stated use; firefighting lacks dedicated turnout gear framing.
Electrical workers and arc protection are mentioned, but this is secondary to the explicit welding use and not independently dominant.
General industrial workwear framing exists but a narrower named sector (welding) is explicitly stated, making it unnecessary as primary.
Oil and gas coverall image label does not indicate vehicle or vessel transport use.
